questions.text: The value of acceleration due to gravity on Earth's surface is approximately:
questions.explanation: The acceleration due to gravity (g) on Earth's surface is approximately 9.8 m/s² or 9.81 m/s².

questions.text: Kepler's third law states that T² ∝ r³.
questions.explanation: Kepler's third law (law of periods): The square of the orbital period is proportional to the cube of the semi-major axis.
G is the universal gravitational constant with value 6.67×10^-11 Nm²/kg²

questions.correctAnswer: The minimum velocity needed for an object to escape from Earth's gravitational field is called escape velocity.
questions.explanation: Escape velocity ve = √(2GM/R) = √(2gR) ≈ 11.2 km/s for Earth.
